name: King & King
description_short: Atlanta-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, with 40 years of experience helping Georgia residents resolve debt and avoid foreclosure.
description_long:
King & King is a bankruptcy law firm headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, with four decades of experience serving residents facing overwhelming debt. The firm was established to provide legal solutions during financially difficult times, and has built its practice around helping thousands of clients navigate the U.S. Bankruptcy Code.

The firm offers comprehensive bankruptcy services across two primary chapters: Chapter 7 (liquidation) and Chapter 13 (reorganization/repayment plans). Additional services include lien stripping, foreclosure prevention, car repossession defense, and wage garnishment relief. They serve clients throughout Georgia and advertise free initial consultations. Remote appointments became available during COVID-19 and continue to be offered alongside in-person visits.

King & King distinguishes itself through longevity and scale—the firm has helped thousands of clients and references a 2014 National Bureau of Economic Research study finding that Chapter 13 bankruptcy filers experience increased annual earnings ($5,562), reduced five-year mortality, and significantly lower foreclosure rates (19.1 percentage point reduction). This data-driven approach to explaining bankruptcy's benefits sets them apart from fear-based messaging.

The firm's main limitations are geographic focus (Georgia-specific practice) and lack of detailed attorney credentials, case outcomes, or fee information on their website. While they emphasize professional service and accessibility, prospective clients will need to call for specifics on attorney experience, success rates, or pricing structures.

Consumers considering bankruptcy should also explore alternatives. Debt relief programs may negotiate settlements for less than owed, while debt consolidation loans can simplify payments into one monthly bill. Credit counseling agencies offer free financial assessments and debt management plans. After bankruptcy, rebuilding credit through secured credit cards and credit builder loans provides a structured path back. Credit repair services can help ensure the bankruptcy filing is accurately reported and outdated items are removed on schedule. Credit monitoring services provide ongoing visibility during the multi-year recovery process. After discharge, qualifying for an installment loan — even a small one with higher rates — can begin rebuilding payment history on your credit report.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: 40 years of established practice history serving Georgia residents
Helped thousands of clients avoid foreclosure and car repossession
Free initial consultation to assess bankruptcy options
Remote appointment options available for clients unable to visit office
Comprehensive service offerings covering Chapter 7, Chapter 13, lien stripping, and wage garnishment relief
Cites peer-reviewed research (NBER 2014 study) showing bankruptcy benefits rather than just anecdotal claims
Professional, courteous staff positioned to make difficult process easier for families
cons: Website lacks attorney credentials, bar status, or individual attorney profiles
No published fee schedules or pricing information available online
No case outcomes, success rates, or client testimonials displayed
Limited to Georgia practice—cannot serve clients in other states
Sparse blog content (only 'Hello world' posts) suggests minimal online engagement or educational resources
best_for: Georgia residents facing foreclosure or home loss who need Chapter 13 reorganization
Individuals with significant unsecured debt seeking Chapter 7 liquidation and fresh start
Car owners at risk of repossession or facing wage garnishment in Georgia
Clients seeking in-person consultation with option for remote accessibility
diagnosis: King & King is best for Georgia residents facing imminent foreclosure, repossession, or wage garnishment who need experienced legal representation to file Chapter 7 or Chapter 13 bankruptcy. The main caveat is that the website provides minimal transparency on attorney qualifications, fees, or case outcomes—prospective clients must call 404.524.6400 to evaluate whether the firm's specific experience and pricing align with their needs.
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
meta_description: Atlanta-based bankruptcy law firm specializing in Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filings, helping Georgia residents resolve debt.
